question.text: A secondary trend in Dow Theory typically lasts:

answer.text: 3 weeks to 3 months, retracing 1/3 to 2/3 of the primary move

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Secondary trends are intermediate corrections/rallies lasting 3 weeks to 3 months, retracing 1/3 to 2/3 of the primary trend move (as per Dow Theory). **The Trap:** Tertiary (minor) trends last days to weeks. Primary trends last 1+ years. Secondary is the intermediate move. **Cross-Exam Context:** Ch 15 NISM XV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Secondary trends often shake out weak hands. They test conviction and reset overbought/oversold conditions before the primary trend resumes.
